<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kundikas]][[ja:水瓶]][[zh-yue:軍持]][[zh:軍持]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kundikas]][[ja:水瓶]][[zh-yue:軍持]][[zh:軍持]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:水瓶]][[zh-yue:軍持]][[zh:軍持]]

{{Otheruses|Water Bottles in Buddhism|Water storage jars for daily use|甕}}
[[File:荒久遺跡出土 灰釉花文浄瓶.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Gray Glazed Floral Patterned Water Jar<br/>{{small|Excavated from Araku Site (Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture). Displayed at Ichihara History Museum.}}]]
'''Water Jar''' (水瓶, ''suibyō'' or ''mizugame'') is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} vessel used to hold [[argha]] (holy water). It is one of the 18 essential possessions that a [[:en:bhikshu|bhikshu]] (monk) must carry. The Sanskrit term "kundī" is translated as '''Gunji''' (軍持) in phonetic transcription. In Buddhist iconography, it is sometimes depicted as an attribute held by deities such as {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Avalokiteśvara|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (Kannon Bodhisattva).

Water jars have been in use since ancient times and can be made of pottery, cast bronze, or other materials. Some examples are designated as [[Cultural Properties]].

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Jar|en|Jar|qid=Q1207302|lt=Jar|1=Jar}}
* {{ill|Vase|en|Vase|qid=Q191851|lt=Vase|1=Vase}}
* {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|en|Aquarius (constellation)|qid=Q10576|lt=Aquarius (constellation)|1=Aquarius (constellation)}}
* {{ill|Aquarian Age|en|Aquarian Age|qid=Q6499537|lt=Aquarian Age|1=Aquarian Age}}

{{buddhism2}}

{{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|水瓶|version=93862087|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:水瓶]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kundikas}}
[[zh:軍持]]
[[zh-yue:軍持]]
{{wikidata link|Q11548974}}
[[Category:Buddha statues]]
[[Category:Containers]]
[[Category:Crafts]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
